title: Post-operative respiratory concern documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting OSA severity, procedure invasiveness, postoperative analgesic context, and clinician-assessed perioperative risk without an automatic disposition or treatment.

source.title: Practice Guidelines for the Perioperative Management of Patients with Obstructive Sleep Apnea
source.population: Adult and pediatric patients older than 1 year with suspected or established obstructive sleep apnea in perioperative settings.
source.setting: Preoperative, intraoperative, and postoperative anesthesia care.
source.exclusions: Exact for record review, patient or family interview, airway examination, prior airway difficulty, OSA symptoms and studies, severity, procedure, and opioid-related risk context. Treatment and disposition recommendations are not mapped.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting OSA severity, procedure invasiveness, postoperative analgesic context, and clinician-assessed perioperative risk without an automatic disposition or treatment.

assessment.text: Supports periodic documented respiratory, cardiovascular, neurologic or mental, temperature, pain, nausea or vomiting, hydration, urine-output, drainage, and bleeding observations.
assessment.rationale: The exact ASA recovery section supports clinician-documented respiratory and cardiovascular status and symptom review after anesthesia; it does not infer a complication or direct treatment.

source.title: Practice Guidelines for Postanesthetic Care
source.population: Patients recovering from anesthesia or sedation, with clinical and procedural qualifications.
source.exclusions: Exact for periodic recovery assessment domains including respiratory and cardiovascular status, mental status, temperature, pain, nausea or vomiting, hydration, urine output, drainage, and bleeding. Treatment recommendations are not mapped; recency is a blocker.

exact_location.heading: Patient assessment and monitoring during recovery

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports periodic documented respiratory, cardiovascular, neurologic or mental, temperature, pain, nausea or vomiting, hydration, urine-output, drainage, and bleeding observations.

assessment.text: Supports transport with appropriate monitoring and support, arrival re-evaluation, verbal report, and documented transfer status.
assessment.rationale: The current ASA standard supports recording an already completed transfer, arrival evaluation, and handover for a postoperative respiratory concern.

source.title: Standards for Postanesthesia Care
source.population: Patients who receive general anesthesia, regional anesthesia, or monitored anesthesia care.
source.setting: PACU or equivalent postanesthesia recovery location.
source.exclusions: Exact for transfer, arrival evaluation, handover, recovery monitoring and records, discharge criteria, and qualified discharge responsibility. It does not generate discharge or treatment decisions.

exact_location.heading: Standards I–II — PACU transfer, arrival evaluation, and handover

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports transport with appropriate monitoring and support, arrival re-evaluation, verbal report, and documented transfer status.

assessment.text: Supports documented ventilation, oxygenation, and level-of-consciousness assessment without specifying dose or generating treatment.
assessment.rationale: This section was reviewed for oxygenation, ventilation, and consciousness documentation but was not mapped because it applies only when neuraxial opioid exposure is established.

source.title: Practice Guidelines for the Prevention, Detection, and Management of Respiratory Depression Associated with Neuraxial Opioid Administration
source.population: Patients receiving neuraxial opioids, with patient-specific respiratory-risk qualifiers.
source.setting: Periprocedural and postprocedural neuraxial opioid care.
source.exclusions: Exact for focused risk history and documented ventilation, oxygenation, and consciousness monitoring. Opioid selection, dose, monitoring frequency, and treatment are not mapped.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documented ventilation, oxygenation, and level-of-consciousness assessment without specifying dose or generating treatment.

assessment.text: Supports documenting OSA severity, procedure invasiveness, postoperative analgesic context, and clinician-assessed perioperative risk without an automatic disposition or treatment.
assessment.rationale: This section was reviewed for OSA-related postoperative risk but was not mapped because the legacy workflow does not establish OSA or the relevant procedure and analgesic context.
